name: Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail
name_original: Parque Megalítico Dólmenes de Las Aceñas de la Borrega – running circular
description: Jog the 4.8-mile Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail, a moderate route through ancient megalithic sites and natural beauty near Valencia de Al
region_intro: Jogging routes around Valencia De Alcántara offer a unique blend of natural beauty and ancient history in Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by impressive granite formations, expansive pine forests, and serene valleys. Many running trails traverse landscapes rich with megalithic monuments, providing a distinctive backdrop for outdoor activity. This diverse terrain ensures a varied experience for runners exploring the area. Best jogging routes around Valencia De Alcántara The most popular jogging route is Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores ancient megalithic sites and granite formations. Another top favourite among local runners is Menir de Água de Cuba loop from Santo António das Areias, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This route features varied terrain and offers views of the surrounding natural landscape. Local runners also love the Rio Sever loop from Santa Maria de Marvão, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Jogging around Valencia De Alcántara is defined by megalithic monuments, granite rock formations, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Valencia De Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 7 reviews. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Valencia De Alcántara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: There are over 30 jogging routes around Valencia de Alcántara, offering a wide range of options for different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: The routes feature diverse terrain, including paths through impressive granite formations, serene pine forests, and expansive valleys. You'll encounter both accessible paths and more challenging sections, often with views of ancient megalithic sites.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the routes in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: The running routes in Valencia de Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Runners often praise the unique blend of natural beauty and historical sites, particularly the ancient dolmens and the varied landscapes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier run?
A: Yes, there are several moderate running paths available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menir de Água de Cuba loop from Santo António das Areias&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5 km path featuring varied terrain and natural landscapes, suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: Are there more challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. Valencia de Alcántara offers 14 difficult routes for experienced runners. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24530362" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;16th century bridge of Portagem – Marvão Walled Town and Castle loop from Santo António das Areias&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 21 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What historical sites or natural landmarks can I see while jogging?
A: Many routes traverse areas rich in history and natural beauty. You can encounter numerous megalithic monuments, such as the well-preserved &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Mellizo-Anta de la Marquesa&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen Datas 1 and 2&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features impressive granite rock formations, especially within the El Berrocal de La Data Natural Monument, and offers views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marvão Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific routes that highlight the megalithic monuments?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its megalithic routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990918730/acena-de-la-borrega-dolmens-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 7.6 km route specifically designed to explore ancient megalithic sites and granite formations, including the Dolmen del Mellizo.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Many of the running routes in Valencia de Alcántara are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24531426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Sever loop from Santa Maria de Marvão&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20132985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Justicia loop from Valencia de Alcántara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: The best time for jogging in Valencia de Alcántara is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the natural landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late evenings more suitable, while winters are generally mild but can be wet.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: While specific regulations can vary, many natural trails in Extremadura are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or specific route descriptions for any restrictions, particularly in protected natural areas.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in the area?
A: For families, routes with moderate difficulty and relatively flat terrain are ideal. The region's well-signposted trails, especially those exploring the megalithic sites, can be a fascinating experience for all ages, offering a blend of exercise and historical discovery. Look for shorter, moderate routes that avoid significant elevation gains.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running routes?
A: Parking availability varies by route. Many trailheads, especially those for popular routes like the Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail, often have designated parking areas nearby. For routes starting from towns or villages, street parking or public car parks may be available. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for parking recommendations.
tips: Undoubtedly two of the most important specimens of the megalithic park and located in a colorful and picturesque landscape of Extremadura.

The Dolmen del Mellizo, also known as Aceña Borrega or Anta de la Marquesa, has a thousand-year history. Although its exact origin is lost in time, it is believed that it was built between the 4th and 3rd millennia BC. This megalith follows the megalithic model known as “anta” in neighboring Portugal. The dolmen chamber is covered by a horizontal slab that protrudes from the roof, and although it is quite worn, the corridor and access to the chamber are still well preserved. In an archaeological excavation carried out in 1985, remains of funerary goods, such as ceramic fragments and arrowheads, were found. The Dolmen del Mellizo is a fascinating place that connects us with the people who built it thousands of years ago. 🌟
One of the two dolmens nicknamed "Cajirón" found in the area of Las Terceriñas a few kilometers from the town of La Aceña de la Borrega.
A few kilometers from the town of La Aceña de la Borrega is this megalithic park with 5 specimens of dolmens that make it one of the most important and most visited in the Extremadura region.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: The Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail is generally considered to be of moderate difficulty for jogging. While some sections are easily accessible, there are a few ups and downs, and the terrain includes a mix of unpaved tracks and paved roads. It's suitable for a range of fitness levels, but a basic level of fitness will make the experience more enjoyable.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter varied terrain on this trail. Approximately 4.55 km consists of unpaved tracks, while about 2.44 km is on paved roads. There are also sections of narrower paths. The presence of granite, from which the dolmens are constructed, suggests firm or rocky ground in places, along with some gentle ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What historical sites and natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in both history and natural beauty. You'll jog past several impressive prehistoric dolmens, including Dolmen Datas 1 and 2, Dolmen del Mellizo-Anta de la Marquesa, Dolmen Cajirón 2, and Dolmen Cajirón 1. Beyond these ancient monuments, you'll enjoy picturesque natural surroundings, including the Regato de los Mellizos stream, ancient chestnut trees, and the unique granite formations of the Berrocal de la Data.

faqs.question: Is the Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail is a circular route, meaning you'll end up back at your starting point without having to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the center of Aceña de la Borrega. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you should look for designated parking within the village or nearby, as it's a common starting point for visitors exploring the dolmens.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the dolmens or the trail?
faqs.answer: The Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail is part of the Valencia de Alcántara Megalithic Site, one of Europe's most important dolmenic complexes. Generally, access to the outdoor trails and the dolmens themselves is free, and no specific permits are required for jogging or hiking. However, it's always good practice to check for any local regulations or temporary restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail meanders through natural landscapes and historical sites, it is generally considered dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is kept on a leash, especially around historical monuments and to respect local wildlife and other visitors. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog the Aceña de la Borrega Dolmens Trail is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions. Summer can be very hot in Extremadura, and while winter can be pleasant, some paths might be muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from the center of Aceña de la Borrega, a small village. You may find basic amenities such as local cafes or small shops there. For more extensive options like restaurants, pubs, or accommodation, you would likely need to visit the nearby town of Valencia de Alcántara.

faqs.question: How can I get to the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options directly to Aceña de la Borrega might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules from larger towns like Valencia de Alcántara or Cáceres. Alternatively, driving and parking in Aceña de la Borrega is often the most convenient way to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, the trail can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to walking or jogging for a few kilometers. The historical dolmens provide an engaging educational element. However, be mindful of the total distance and elevation changes, and ensure children are appropriately equipped for the conditions.
